description: Marker identified in North American Indigenous lineages.
Marker linked to South American Indigenous groups.
Marker identified with historic Gullah/Geechee populations of the US Atlantic coast.
Marker associated with Caribbean-descent African American lineages.
Marker related to Central/West African origins common across African American populations.
Marker associated with Central American Indigenous populations, particularly in Mexico and Guatemala.
Marker deeply connected to Amazonian Indigenous lineages.
Variant associated with hair texture common in diverse West African populations.
Bantu diagnostic SNP - informative for West-Central and Eastern African Bantu-speaking populations.
Marker identified with phenotypic variation in East African Bantu populations.
Sickle Cell Trait marker, highly informative for West African ancestry.
Marker associated with elevated frequency in Indigenous American populations.
Insular Southeast Asian Marker - diagnostic for Austronesian speaking populations of the Philippines and Indonesia.
Mainland Southeast Asian Marker - informative for Tai-Kadai and Austroasiatic speaking populations.
Tibeto-Burman Border Marker - diagnostic for populations along the Himalayas and Indo-Burma border.
North Mesopotamian Marker - highly informative for Kurdish and Northern Fertile Crescent populations.
South Caucasus/Azeri Marker - diagnostic for populations of the Caucasus and Northwestern Iran.
Indo-Iranian Border Marker - informative for populations of the Northwest Subcontinent and Eastern Plateau.
Hazaragi/Central Asian Marker - diagnostic for the unique admixed populations of central Afghanistan and the Silk Road.
ALDH2 Alcohol Sensitivity Variant - globally unique to East Asian populations.
Southeast Asian Informative Marker - highly prevalent in Austroasiatic and Austronesian speaking populations.
South Asian Specific Marker - informative for distinguishing Indo-European speaking populations of the subcontinent.
Dravidian Ancestry Marker - highly diagnostic for Southern Indian and Sri Lankan populations.
West Asian/Middle Eastern Informative Marker - diagnostic for Afroasiatic and Indo-Iranian speaking populations of the Near East.
Peninsular Arabian Marker - highly informative for populations of the Arabian Peninsula.
Levantine Endemic Marker - diagnostic for the indigenous populations of the Levant.
Khoisan Ancestry Marker - highly diagnostic for the indigenous Khoe-San populations of Southern Africa.
Nilotic Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Nilotic-speaking populations of the Nile Valley and East Africa.
Cushitic Ancestry Marker - informative for Afroasiatic-speaking populations of the Horn of Africa.
Central African Hunter-Gatherer Marker - diagnostic for Pygmy populations of the Congo Basin.
Upper Guinea Ancestry Marker - informative for populations of the Senegambia and Sierra Leone region.
Bight of Benin Ancestry Marker - highly informative for Gbe and Yoruboid speaking populations.
East African Bantu Marker - informative for Bantu-speaking populations of the Great Lakes region.
Southern Bantu Ancestry Marker - informative for Nguni and Sotho-Tswana speaking populations.
Berber Ancestry Marker - highly informative for the indigenous Amazigh populations of North Africa.
Sahelian Ancestry Marker - informative for populations of the Sahel and Southern Sahara.
Nilo-Saharan Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for populations of the Lake Chad Basin and Darfur.
Gur Ancestry Marker - informative for populations of the Voltaic region (Burkina Faso, Northern Ghana).
Ethiosemitic Ancestry Marker - informative for the Semitic-speaking populations of the Ethiopian Highlands.
SLC24A5 variant - highly informative for West Eurasian and South Asian populations.
EDAR variant - diagnostic for East Asian and Native American populations, associated with hair and dental traits.
HERC2 variant - informative for West Eurasian and Middle Eastern populations, associated with eye color.
ABCC11 variant - highly informative for East Asian, Native American, and Oceanian populations.
Central Asian Steppe Marker - diagnostic for the admixed populations of the Silk Road.
Ancestral South Indian (ASI) Marker - diagnostic for the indigenous populations of the Indian subcontinent.
Pan-American Indigenous Marker - highly diagnostic for pre-Columbian populations of the Americas.
Melanesian Specific Marker - diagnostic for the unique genetic lineages of the Bismarck Archipelago and Solomon Islands.
IRF4 variant - highly informative for European populations, associated with skin and hair pigmentation.
SLC45A2 variant - diagnostic for European ancestry, specifically Northern and Western branches.
HERC2/OCA2 region marker - highly informative for Middle Eastern and Mediterranean populations.
East Asian Core Marker - highly diagnostic for Sinitic and Japonic populations.
High-Altitude Adaptation Marker - informative for Tibetan and Central Asian populations.
Kru Ancestry Marker - informative for Kru-speaking populations of the Liberian coast.
Bassa Ancestry Marker - informative for Kru-speaking populations of Liberia.
Kpelle Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Mande-speaking populations of Liberia and Guinea.
Bakongo Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for populations of the former Kingdom of Kongo.
Edo Ancestry Marker - informative for populations of the former Benin Empire (Nigeria).
Mende Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Mande-speaking populations of Sierra Leone.
Limba Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for indigenous populations of Sierra Leone.
Baluba Ancestry Marker - informative for Luba populations of the Democratic Republic of Congo.
Chokwe Ancestry Marker - informative for populations of Angola, DRC, and Zambia.
Lozi Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for populations of the Barotse Floodplain (Zambia).
Bemba Ancestry Marker - informative for Bantu populations of Zambia and DRC.
Tonga Ancestry Marker - informative for Bantu populations of the Zambezi Valley.
Chewa Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Maravi populations of Malawi and Zambia.
Makua Ancestry Marker - informative for Bantu populations of Northern Mozambique.
High significance Nigerian Marker - Fulani focus.
High significance Nigerian Marker - Edo focus.
High significance Nigerian Marker - Ibibio focus.
Duffy Null Phenotype. Essential for tracking the Bantu contribution to the Malagasy gene pool.
SLC24A5 variant. Used here to distinguish between the 'pure' African and the admixed Malagasy profiles.

IRF5 - informative for distinguishing Native Americans from other populations.
HFE - inverse marker, presence signals European ancestry.

FADS2 - associated with fatty acid metabolism in Native American populations.
Beringian Standstill Proxy - diagnostic for separating Indigenous American from later Asian migrations.
SLC16A11 - high frequency in Native American populations, associated with diabetes risk.

Andean Ancestry Marker - informative for populations from the Andes region.
Sherbro Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for coastal populations of Sierra Leone.

Maori Ancestry Marker - informative for Polynesian populations from New Zealand.
Fijian Ancestry Marker - informative for populations from Fiji.
Tongan Ancestry Marker - informative for populations from Tonga.
Tahitian Ancestry Marker - informative for populations from Tahiti.
Cook Islander Ancestry Marker - informative for populations from the Cook Islands.

Aboriginal Australian Marker - diagnostic for ancient Sahul genetic signatures.
Samoan Ancestry Marker - informative for western Polynesian populations.
Melanesian Ancestry Marker - informative for populations from Vanuatu and the Solomon Islands.
Micronesian Ancestry Marker - informative for populations of the Mariana and Caroline Islands.
Loma Ancestry Marker - informative for Mande-speaking populations of Guinea and Liberia.
SLC24A5 variant - associated with lighter skin pigmentation, prevalent in South Asian populations.
South Asian Ancestry Marker - informative for populations from the Indian subcontinent.
South Asian Ancestry Marker - informative for Indo-Aryan and Dravidian populations.
Shona Ancestry Marker - informative for populations of Zimbabwe and Mozambique.
Tsonga Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Tswa-Ronga populations of South Africa and Mozambique.
Ndebele Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Nguni populations of Zimbabwe and South Africa.
Punjabi Ancestry Marker - informative for Northern Indo-Aryan populations.
Bamileke Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Grassfields Bantu populations of Cameroon.
EUR/MENA Tie-Breaker - Highly specific to Northern and Central Europe, rare across the Mediterranean.
MENA/EUR Tie-Breaker - Frequent in the Levant and Anatolia, drops off sharply in Southern Europe.
NAFR/EUR Tie-Breaker - Distinctive Maghreb marker, filters out false Iberian/Southern European overlap.
AFR/MENA Tie-Breaker - Fixed in Sub-Saharan populations, prevents Middle Eastern noise from registering as African.
MENA/AFR Tie-Breaker - Distinctive to the Arabian Peninsula, filters out false Horn of Africa overlap.
NAFR/AFR Tie-Breaker - Prevents Sahel/Saharan border populations from misclassifying as strictly Sub-Saharan.
EAS/AMR Tie-Breaker - Highly prevalent in Siberia and East Asia, sharply reduced in the Americas.
AMR/EAS Tie-Breaker - Post-migration mutation unique to the Americas, prevents false East Asian trace results.
EAS/AMR Tie-Breaker - Diagnostic for East Asian coastal populations, effectively absent in Native Americans.
SAS/MENA Tie-Breaker - Highly specific to the Indian subcontinent, prevents misclassification as Iranian/Middle Eastern.
MENA/SAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors populations to the Iranian plateau and West Asia, filtering out South Asian noise.
SAS/EAS Tie-Breaker - Differentiates populations south of the Himalayas from East/Southeast Asian groups.
OCE/EAS Tie-Breaker - Deep Sahul lineage marker, strongly filters out Austronesian/East Asian overlap.
EAS/OCE Tie-Breaker - Common across Southeast Asia, drops sharply in unadmixed Melanesian/Papuan groups.
EUR/SAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors Western hunter-gatherer ancestry, drops sharply in South Asian populations.
SAS/EUR Tie-Breaker - Diagnostic for Ancestral South Indian (ASI) components, effectively absent in Europe.
EAS/SAS Tie-Breaker - Common in Tibeto-Burman populations, filters out false East Asian noise in South Asia.
SAS/EAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors Indo-Aryan genetics, strongly separating the subcontinent from East Asia.
NAFR/MENA Tie-Breaker - Deep autochthonous Maghrebi marker, sharply reduces false Middle Eastern overlap.
MENA/NAFR Tie-Breaker - Natufian-derived Levantine marker, preventing Egyptian/North African misclassification.
OCE/AFR Tie-Breaker - Denisovan-introgressed lineage marker, entirely absent in Sub-Saharan Africa.
AMR/OCE Tie-Breaker - Ancient Beringian mutation, prevents false Papuan/Melanesian signals in Amazonian groups.
OCE/AMR Tie-Breaker - Deep Sahul lineage, effectively absent in indigenous populations of the Americas.
AFR/EAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors deep Sub-Saharan lineages against Southeast Asian introgression.
EAS/AFR Tie-Breaker - Diagnostic for Austronesian expansion markers, filtering out African overlap.
EUR/EAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors Western Eurasian genetics against Siberian/Turkic gene flow.
EAS/EUR Tie-Breaker - Highly specific to Eastern Eurasian Steppe populations.
MENA/SAS Tie-Breaker - Anchors Iranian Plateau genetics against Indo-Aryan overlap.
SAS/MENA Tie-Breaker - Diagnostic for deeper South Asian (AASI) lineages.
AMR/EUR Tie-Breaker - Strictly isolates Indigenous American DNA from Iberian/European introgression.
EUR/AMR Tie-Breaker - European-specific marker that frequently appears in admixed Latino populations.
NAFR/AFR Tie-Breaker - Anchors indigenous Berber/Maghrebi components against Sub-Saharan gene flow.
AFR/NAFR Tie-Breaker - Sub-Saharan specific marker to filter out noise from the Sahel corridor.
West African Ancestry Marker - highly informative for Niger-Congo speaking populations.
Mandinka/Wolof Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Upper Guinea and Senegambian populations.
East African/Horn of Africa Marker - informative for Cushitic and Ethiosemitic populations.
Khoisan Ancestry Marker - highly diagnostic for Southern African hunter-gatherer populations.
Central African Pygmy Marker - informative for Mbuti and Biaka genetic signatures.
Nilotic Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Nilo-Saharan speaking populations of East Africa.
Horn of Africa Regional Marker - informative for Afroasiatic populations of the Horn.
Southern Bantu Ancestry Marker - informative for Nguni and Sotho-Tswana populations.
Central/Western Bantu Ancestry Marker - informative for populations of the Congo Basin.
Dan Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Mande-speaking populations of Liberia and Ivory Coast.
Mano Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Mande-speaking populations of Liberia.
Ovimbundu Ancestry Marker - informative for Umbundu-speaking populations of Angola.
Yao Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for populations of the Lake Malawi region.
Maasai Ancestry Marker - informative for Nilotic pastoralist populations of East Africa.
Nigerian Regional Marker - informative for populations of the Niger-Benue confluence.
Nubian Ancestry Marker - diagnostic for Nilo-Saharan populations of the Nile Valley.
Sudanese Ancestry Marker - informative for Nilotic populations of South Sudan and Sudan.
Akan Ancestry Marker - highly informative for populations of the Gold Coast and Ivory Coast.
Central African Bantu Marker - diagnostic for populations of the Congo Basin and Cameroon.
Khoe-San Specific Marker - highly diagnostic for indigenous Southern African hunter-gatherer populations.
Horn of Africa Marker - informative for Cushitic and Ethiosemitic populations of East Africa.
APOL1 gene variant - highly prevalent in West African populations, associated with kidney disease risk and trypanosomiasis resistance.
African Informative Marker - general sub-Saharan African signal.
African Informative Marker - strong differentiation from non-African populations.
African Informative Marker - strong sub-Saharan African signal.
African Informative Marker - useful for distinguishing African from Middle Eastern/North African populations.
African Informative Marker - strong sub-Saharan African signal with high differentiation.
African Informative Marker - highly prevalent in Bantu-speaking populations.
